summary:
The process describes early signs moving into more advanced stages of periodontal disease characterized by deepening spaces due to loss of connective tissue support from both soft tissues and alveolar bones as part of inflammatory responses within the oral cavity.
To refine it further while adhering strictly:

Apical migration of the epithelial attachment coupled with marginal gingival recession signifies advancing periodontal pathology through increased space development between tooth structures and their surrounding soft tissues, reflecting deteriorating health at multiple anatomical levels including reduced supportive bony structure integrity. This change is consistent with progressing inflammation-driven destruction characteristic of established periodontitis conditions. To ensure clarity without extraneous details,

The apical shift of the epithelial attachment alongside marginal gingival recession signals worsening periodontal condition marked by growing interdental spacing indicating potential for significant structural degradation involving both attached gingiva and underlying jawbone supporting dental roots. These changes are hallmark features pointing strongly towards active or developing chronic periodontitis processes affecting overall dentition stability negatively over time if left untreated.
